% Float wrapper around the auto-generated tabular (tables/main_results.tex).
% The tabular file is produced verbatim by scripts/make_tables.py from
% results/summary.json and is the single source of truth; this wrapper only adds
% the float, caption, and label. Do not hand-edit the numbers in main_results.tex.
\begin{table}[htbp]
    \centering
    \caption{Reported-scale results (\texttt{configs/full.yaml}; $120$ graphs, $n\le16$, depth-1, budget $40$), per warm-start policy: final approximation ratio (exact, against a brute-force MaxCut oracle) with a $\pm95\%$ confidence interval, mean queries-to-target, and target hit rate. The graph-conditioned, topology, and spectral policies are statistically indistinguishable on approximation ratio (means differ by $\sim10^{-4}$, far inside the $\pm0.009$ interval); all structure-aware policies improve on random by $\approx0.052$ ($+7.1\%$), which exceeds the confidence interval. The target ratio ($0.95$) exceeds the attainable depth-1 optimum for these graphs, so no policy hits it; consequently queries-to-target defaults to the budget ($40$) and the hit rate is $0$ for every policy, and the query advantage is reported from the frontier (Sec.~\ref{sec:query_efficiency}, Fig.~\ref{fig:frontier}) rather than from this column.}
    \label{tab:main}
    \input{tables/main_results.tex}
\end{table}
